novel_features.name: Price history
novel_features.description: See a product's recorded price trend and its lowest-ever price from your local store.
novel_features.rationale: Daraz shows only today's price; recording snapshots over runs is only possible with the local SQLite store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to judge whether a current price is actually low for that item, not just discounted on paper.

novel_features.name: Price watch
novel_features.description: Record current prices for every item matching a search into the local store so price history builds over time.
novel_features.rationale: Requires persisting price snapshots locally across runs; no scraper keeps history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run periodically on a query you care about; then price-history shows the trend for any item it captured.

novel_features.name: Saved-search change alerts
novel_features.description: Diff a saved search against its last local snapshot to show new listings and price moves since you last checked.
novel_features.rationale: Needs a stored prior snapshot of the query's results to diff against; a single API call cannot do this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to monitor a market over time instead of re-reading a full result list each run.

novel_features.name: Real-deal ranking
novel_features.description: Rank a search by a composite of discount, rating, and units sold to surface genuinely good deals.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local join and scoring across every scanned item, not the site's nominal % off ordering.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the goal is the best-value item for a query, not raw listing order.

novel_features.name: Fake-discount / value detector
novel_features.description: Flag inflated original-price discounts by comparing each item's claimed original price to the local median for that query.
novel_features.rationale: Detecting a fake markdown needs the query's market median computed locally across all results.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to avoid misleading discounts; it is the inverse of deals.

novel_features.name: Cross-seller compare
novel_features.description: Find the same item across sellers and show the cheapest and best-rated side by side.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local join of the same query across sellers; no single endpoint returns this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to pick the best seller/listing for an item before buying.

novel_features.name: Seller scorecard
novel_features.description: Aggregate a seller's catalog from the local store: average rating, price range, listing count, discount pattern.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregating a seller's catalog requires the synced local store of their listings.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to vet a seller's track record before trusting a listing.

narrative.headline: Every Daraz product search, plus a local price database, real-deal ranking, and drop alerts no Daraz scraper has.
narrative.value_prop: daraz-pp-cli searches Daraz.pk listings, reads reviews, and extracts product detail like any scraper — then keeps it in a local SQLite store so it can do what one-shot scrapers cannot: track price history (price-history), rank genuine deals over fake discounts (deals, value), diff a saved search over time (since), and vet sellers (seller stats). Read-only, no login required.

quickstart.comment: Health check — confirms daraz.pk is reachable; needs no credentials.
Core search with a PKR price range, cheapest first.
Read recent reviews for a product by its item ID.

troubleshoots.symptom: Empty results for a query that has products on the site
troubleshoots.fix: Drop filters and retry: daraz-pp-cli products --query "<keyword>" — then re-add --price.
Populate the local store first: daraz-pp-cli watch <itemId> (for price-history) or run the same search once before since.
Confirm the numeric item ID from a product URL (the digits after -i in .../products/...-i<itemId>.html).

narrative.when_to_use: Use daraz-pp-cli for shopper-side Daraz.pk research: finding products, comparing prices and sellers, reading reviews, and tracking whether a price is genuinely low over time. It is ideal for agents that need structured, filterable product data and offline price intelligence from a single keyword.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to place orders, manage a cart, or checkout — it is read-only.
Do not use it for order tracking, wishlists, or account actions that require a logged-in Daraz session.
Do not use it for seller/merchant operations — that is the separate Daraz Open Platform.
Do not use it for non-Pakistan marketplaces; it targets daraz.pk.

recipes.title: Agent-friendly search with field selection
recipes.explanation: deals returns a flat, ranked array of listings, so --select trims each row to just the fields an agent needs and keeps payloads small.

recipes.title: Find the best-value items, not the biggest fake discount
recipes.explanation: Ranks by a discount-times-rating-times-sales composite computed locally.

recipes.title: Start tracking a query's prices
recipes.explanation: Records current prices for the search so price-history builds a real trend over time.

recipes.title: See what changed in a market since last check
recipes.explanation: Diffs the current search against the last local snapshot for new listings and price moves.

trigger_phrases: is this a good deal on daraz
